choices: Population: rows[] entries in /home/user/patientwords-engine/urgency_shift.json with model=='gemma-2-2b-it' (122 rows: 119 from Tier A batch pairs_20260707T171223Z, 3 from Tier B batch pairs_20260710T011743Z).

Holdout rule implemented exactly as: exclude a phrase iff its row belongs to a Tier B batch (carries a tierb_split assignment; batches after the 2026-07-09 registration) AND int(sha1(clinical_prompt.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest(),16) % 10 == 0. Scoped to Tier B because preregistration_tierB.md Amendment 1 assigns splits only to Tier B pairs ('every Tier B pair is assigned to an analysis split'). Validation: this rule reproduces the file's tierb_split explore/holdout labels 2037/2037. Result: 0 of the 122 phrases excluded (the 3 Tier B rows hash nonzero). Ambiguity noted: 11 Tier A phrases also hash to 0 mod 10; I retained them since the sealed holdout does not apply to Tier A — an unscoped reading of the rule would instead give n=111.

Penalty: used the per-row language_penalty field, which I verified in trace outputs equals probabilities.patient − probabilities.clinical, i.e. P(clinical target continuation token | patient wording) − P(same token | clinical wording), matching the written definition. It is NOT p_top_patient − p_top_clinical (those are argmax-token probabilities). mean_penalty_pp = 100 × mean over deduped phrases = -6.2251 → -6.2.
Dedup: one record per (model, clinical_prompt) by exact string match; all 122 prompts were unique for this model, so mean-of-duplicate probabilities and majority-vote flip collapse were no-ops.

Downgrade/upgrade: used the per-row flip_class classification field already present (downgrade/upgrade/lateral/uninformative; null when top prediction did not flip), majority-voted per phrase (trivial with no duplicates). Deduped counts: downgrade 6, upgrade 2 (also lateral 15, uninformative 18, no-flip 81).

Field-name mapping: the prompt's prob_clinical/prob_patient correspond to row fields p_top_clinical/p_top_patient (top-token probabilities under each wording); verified against predictive_spread[...][0][1] in the trace files.

Sample check procedure: random.Random(42).sample over the 122 deduped phrases in file (first-occurrence) order; joined to trace_out/<batch>__gemma-2-2b-it/batch_summary.part_01.json via results[i]['index']; compared clinical_prompt text, p_top_clinical, p_top_patient, language_penalty, and language_penalty vs probabilities.patient−probabilities.clinical with tolerance 0.0005 (row values are rounded to 3–4 decimals).
gemma-2-2b-it is a logits-backend model (backend 'logits', source_set null); its trace dirs are suffixed __gemma-2-2b-it and each batch had a single batch_summary.part_01.json.
